mission.title: Install and Configure Kube Vip on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kube-Vip provides a highly available virtual IP and load-balancer for Kubernetes control planes and services. Installing Kube-Vip simplifies the setup of HA Kubernetes clusters, making it suitable for various environments including bare-metal and edge devices.

steps.title: Add the Kube-Vip Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Kube-Vip Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kube-vip https://kube-vip.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kube-Vip using Helm
steps.description:
Install Kube-Vip in the `kube-vip` namespace, creating the namespace if it doesn't exist:
```bash
helm install kube-vip kube-vip/kube-vip --namespace kube-vip --create-namespace --version v1.0.4
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Kube-Vip pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-vip
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the Kube-Vip deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to open the deployment configuration:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment kube-vip -n kube-vip
```
Add the following resource limits under the `spec.containers` section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Expose Kube-Vip service
steps.description:
To access the Kube-Vip service, you can port-forward it to your local machine:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/kube-vip -n kube-vip 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kube-Vip will show the Kube-Vip pods in the `kube-vip` namespace as running. You will also be able to access the service via port-forwarding on your local machine.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kube-vip https://kube-vip.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install kube-vip kube-vip/kube-vip --namespace kube-vip --create-namespace --version v1.0.4

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and have installed Helm and kubectl.
